headline: How The Boston Beer Company (SAM) Narrative Is Shifting With New Targets And Guidance
summary: The updated fair value estimate for Boston Beer Company has shifted from US$239.36 to US$232.70, signaling a modest reset in what some models suggest the shares may be worth. This change comes as Street research splits between higher and lower price targets, reflecting differing views on how much current margins, reinvestment, and category headwinds are already incorporated into the stock. As you read on, you will see how these moving targets shape the evolving analyst narrative and what that...

headline: Private Markets’ Software Pain Is About to Get a Lot Worse
summary: (Bloomberg) -- The software problem roiling private markets is about to face a big new test. A wall of debt maturities is looming for the industry just as artificial intelligence threatens to upend entire businesses in what’s been dubbed the SaaSpocalypse.More than $330 billion of high yield, leveraged loan and business development company-linked software and technology debt is coming due for repayment through 2028, a chunk of it tied to firms owned by private markets. headline: Citigroup Gears Up For Q1 Print; Here Are The Recent Forecast Changes From Wall Street's Most Accurate Analysts

headline: Goldman Sachs Raises Citigroup (C) Target Ahead of Q1 Banking Readout
summary: Citigroup Inc. (NYSE:C) is included among the 13 Bank Stocks with Highest Dividends. On April 6, Goldman Sachs analyst Richard Ramsden raised the firm’s price target on Citigroup Inc. (NYSE:C) to $137 from $123, while keeping a Buy rating on the stock. The change came as part of a broader note looking ahead to Q1 […]
